one_time_description: Trust claim settlement related to 1986 oil and gas claim against Her Majesty the Queen, settlement executed March 29, 2023
economic_notes: Government business enterprises: Bison Auto Stop Ltd. (100%) and Saddle Lake Building Supplies Ltd. (100%). Business partnerships: Saddle Lake Smoke Eaters LP (100%), Seven Lakes LP (8.33%), Pimee Well Servicing LP (16.67%). Long term investments in Pimee Well Servicing Ltd. and United Farmers of Alberta. GBE combined revenue $10,437,525; business partnership combined revenue $13,002,228.
notes: Audit is qualified due to inability to obtain sufficient audit evidence on investments in government business enterprises, business partnerships and long term investments accounted for by modified equity method - qualification applies to both 2023 and 2022. One-time trust claim settlement of $9,414,970 recognized as receivable at year-end (received subsequent to year-end). Fiscal year is March 31, 2023 despite document header saying 2023. Three consolidated subsidiaries: 544526 Alberta Ltd., The Saddle Lake Wah-Koh-To-Win Society, 1786228 Alberta Ltd. Two GBEs (Bison Auto Stop Ltd., Saddle Lake Building Supplies Ltd.) and three business partnerships accounted for by modified equity method. Deferred revenue of $48.3M is very large relative to total liabilities. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $105,346,977 + ISC Health $9,136,040 + Province of Alberta $5,859,479 + ISETP $2,937,926 + YESS $1,085,984 + FNDF $1,364,357 + CMHC $1,529,015 + Child Tax Benefit $483,336 = $127,743,114 less lapsed funding $12,897 = approximately $127,730,217 before netting carried forward items; OSR reported as Other revenue line only. Annual debt service reflects only long term debt repayments per cash flow; capital lease repayments of $95,497 additional. Expenses_health includes Health Services $12,376,247 + Other Health $14,839.
